title: Backup, Point-in-Time Recovery, Rebuild, and Disaster Reconciliation · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Isolated Restore
summary: Backups exist but are untested, feed content is assumed refetchable, reader state shares no recovery objective, restore replays workers early, and corruption remains hidden. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go isolated restore increment in rehearsed feed-service recovery system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Restore into isolation, validate schema and application versions, reconcile counts and invariants, and prevent outbound fetching during inspection.
Verify scheduled backups, WAL continuity, encryption, access, checksums, catalog, age, capacity, and restore eligibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Isolated Restore · Theory Feed Go Isolated Restore
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9045 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove data-class objectives, backup eligibility, isolated restore, safe resume order, and disaster drills; browser Go must remain deterministic and free of network, database, process, and host effects; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Restore into isolation, validate schema and application versions, reconcile counts and invariants, and prevent outbound fetching during inspection.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Feed Go Isolated Restore contract for the supplied data.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-go-isolated-restore-1dw19i(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_feed_go_isolated_restore_1dw19i\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?dataClasses < 7)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?restoredClasses != dataClasses)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?drills < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unreconciledInvariants > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The decision applies Feed Go Isolated Restore to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Feed Go Isolated Restore.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using restore and isolation plus specific case evidence.
